steps.title: Compare the two ships
steps.content: Both the Dreadnought and the Caledonia had three decks, but only the Caledonia was ranked first-rate. Therefore, having three decks was not enough by itself to make a ship first-rate.

steps.title: Avoid claims beyond the data
steps.content: Choice B is contradicted because the Dreadnought had 98 cannons but was not first-rate. Choice C applies the first-rate crew range to a non-first-rate ship. Choice D invents a crew need above 875. The answer is A.
